title: Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act
short_title: Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act
plain_summary: This bill lets a group called the National Medal of Honor Museum Foundation build a special monument on the National Mall in Washington DC. The monument will honor people who received the Medal of Honor, which is the highest military award for bravery. Usually, no new monuments are allowed in the most important part of the National Mall near the Lincoln Memorial, but this bill makes an exception. The group still has to follow all the other rules for building monuments on federal land.
last_action_text: Committee on Energy and Natural Resources Subcommittee on National Parks. Hearings held.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Hershel Woody Williams National Medal of Honor Monument Location Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. In two thousand twenty one, Congress passed a law and the President signed it. This law gave permission to the National Medal of Honor Museum Foundation to build a memorial in Washington D.C. to honor the brave acts and sacrifices of Medal of Honor winners. President Abraham Lincoln created the Medal of Honor one hundred sixty years ago on March twenty fifth, eighteen sixty three. This medal is part of Lincoln's lasting legacy. In nineteen ninety one, President George H.W. Bush made March twenty fifth National Medal of Honor Day every year. The Medal of Honor is given to people in all branches of the military. It is our country's highest award for brave acts in combat. The number of living Medal of Honor winners keeps getting smaller. This makes it more important to honor them and inspire future generations. Hershel Woody Williams was the last World War Two Medal of Honor winner still alive. He died in two thousand twenty two and his body was placed in honor at the United States Capitol. The Medal of Honor represents the best of our country. It goes to ordinary people who did extraordinary things beyond what was expected and became heroes. These examples inspire all citizens and spark the spirit of America in us. The Medal of Honor is so important to our nation and history that it deserves to be represented on the National Mall. Placing the National Medal of Honor Monument near the Lincoln Memorial would respectfully continue his legacy and show what ordinary people can do when they work for the greater good.

sections.title: Section 3  National Medal of Honor Monument Location
sections.text: The memorial that was approved by an earlier law must be built in the Reserve area. The Reserve area is a special part of the National Mall defined by federal law. Even though there is usually a rule that prevents new memorials in this area, this monument is allowed to be built there anyway. All other rules about building commemorative works will still apply to this monument. These rules are found in a federal law commonly called the Commemorative Works Act.
